prose:
Method provenance (fukui-reactivity, phenol)
Property: Condensed Fukui indices f + (electrophilic), f − (nucleophilic), f 0, and dual descriptor (dimensionless)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`, density-fitted) as implemented in PySCF 2.x (via chemkit)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (phenol.xyz) taken as-is; single-point calculations were performed for the neutral (N), cationic (N–1, charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anionic (N+1, charge –1, multiplicity 2) states — no additional geometry optimization (`single-point//file geometry`).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation model).
Total charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the parent; +1 doublet and −1 doublet for the N±1 states, as required by the Fukui protocol.
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged (integrity.status = "ok").
Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Backend / software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF DFT engine). Live engine output was written during the run to the standard chemkit logfile `<cwd>/fukui_<timestamp>.out`; the structured result JSON resides at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpjsq3swpb/live.json.
